Ad Text:
Why UKG: 

At UKG, the work you do matters. The code you ship, the decisions you make, and the care you show a customer all add up to real impact. Today, tens of millions of workers start and end their days with our workforce operating platform. Helping people get paid, grow in their careers, and shape the future of their industries. That’s what we do.

We never stop learning. We never stop challenging the norm. We push for better, and we celebrate the wins along the way. Here, you’ll get flexibility that’s real, benefits you can count on, and a team that succeeds together. Because at UKG, your work matters—and so do you.

About the Role:  

We are seeking a strategic and customer-facing player-coach senior individual contributor to lead value-based selling across APAC, with a primary focus on Australia and New Zealand. Reporting to the Global Value Engineering team, this role partners closely with the GVP & GM, APAC, along with regional Sales, Solution Consulting, Customer Success, and Marketing leaders. You will support strategic new-logo, expansion, and retention opportunities by shaping account strategy, developing differentiated value perspectives, and building compelling business cases and value-based proposals that accelerate growth and strengthen customer confidence.

As value advisor, you will personally be involved in high-impact customer engagements while coaching account teams, solution consultants, and regional stakeholders on value-selling approaches, executive messaging, and business case discipline. The ideal candidate brings strong executive presence, financial acumen, analytical rigor, and the ability to influence complex enterprise sales cycles in a collaborative, fast-moving environment.


Key Responsibilities


Regional Value Leadership & Account Partnership
• Partner with Sales leadership, account teams, and solution consultants to shape account strategy and embed value hypotheses early in pursuit cycles.
• Prioritize and personally lead high-impact engagements across strategic new-logo, expansion, and renewal opportunities, while flexing across broader APAC as business needs evolve.
• Identify key buying personas across HR, Finance, IT, Operations, and business leadership, and tailor value messaging to each stakeholder group.
• Develop executive-ready value narratives aligned to customer priorities, regional market dynamics, and UKG solution capabilities.
Business Case Development & Financial Modeling
• Lead discovery sessions to assess current-state challenges, baseline KPIs, and value levers across workforce, operational, and business processes.
• Build financial models that quantify ROI, TCO, payback period, productivity gains, risk reduction, and broader business impact.
• Translate qualitative insights and process findings into quantified recommendations that resonate with executive and financial decision-makers.
Executive Engagement & Storytelling
• Facilitate executive-level conversations with stakeholders across HR, Finance, IT, Operations, and senior business leadership.
• Deliver clear, data-backed value stories that connect technology investments to strategic priorities, transformation goals, and measurable outcomes.
• Create polished customer-facing materials, including points of view, business cases, value-based proposals, presentations, and white papers.
Customer Value Realization & Lifecycle Support
• Partner with Customer Success to measure post-sale value realization, validate outcomes against original business cases, and support renewal and expansion narratives.
• Capture customer benchmarks, success stories, and best practices to strengthen future pursuits, customer conversations, and reusable value assets.
Player-Coach Enablement & Practice Development
• Coach Sales, Solution Consulting, and Customer Success teams on discovery, value hypothesis development, financial modeling, and executive messaging.
• Lead by example on complex opportunities while raising regional capability through deal coaching, self-service tools, templates, and best practices.
• Collaborate with Product, Marketing, Customer Success, and the global Value Engineering team to refine value messaging, assets, and methodologies for APAC.
• Share field insights and patterns to strengthen VE methodologies, analytics, and communication across the broader team.

About the You: 


• Bachelor's degree in business, finance, economics, MIS, engineering, or a related field; advanced degree preferred.
• 8 - 15+ years of experience in Value Engineering, management consulting, financial analysis, strategic presales, or a related role.

Company Overview:

UKG is the Workforce Operating Platform that puts workforce understanding to work. With the world's largest collection of workforce insights, and people-first AI, our ability to reveal unseen ways to build trust, amplify productivity, and empower talent, is unmatched. It's this expertise that equips our customers with the intelligence to solve any challenge in any industry — because great organizations know their workforce is their competitive edge. Learn more at ukg.com.

UKG is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and is committed to promoting diversity and inclusion in the workplace, including the recruitment process.

Data Governance Manager

Fulltime Permanent

Hybrid, Darlinghurst, Sydney, NSW

Reporting to: IT Director

About you


You are a strategic and collaborative data governance professional who thrives in complex, research-driven environments. You bring a strong balance of governance rigour and practical problem solving, with the ability to translate policy into meaningful, usable frameworks for researchers and technical teams alike. Passionate about enabling high quality, ethical and secure use of data, you enjoy working at the intersection of science, technology and compliance. Above all, you value partnership, clarity and purpose in the work you do.

The role

Reporting to the IT Director, the Data Governance Manager will lead the development and implementation of the Institute's organisation wide data governance strategy. This is a newly established role, created in response to the rapid growth and scale of our research data environment, which currently manages approximately 1.5 petabytes of diverse and complex data assets.

In this pivotal position, you will establish clear accountability for data stewardship, design scalable governance frameworks, and ensure compliance with legal, ethical and funding obligations. You will work closely with researchers, IT teams and executive leaders to enable secure, efficient and transparent data sharing; supporting data driven discovery while reducing administrative burden across the Institute. A Data Governance Officer role will be introduced in the future and will report to you once established.

What we will trust you with  
Leading the development and execution of the Institute's data governance strategy, aligned with research objectives and regulatory requirements
Designing and implementing institute-wide data governance frameworks, policies and standards that support current and future data growth
Establishing and chairing cross-functional data governance committees to drive accountability and consistency
Defining data ownership, stewardship roles and escalation pathways across research divisions and operational teams
Developing and enforcing data quality standards, metadata frameworks and data catalogues to improve data discoverability and reproducibility
Overseeing data security, access controls, classification schemes and incident response processes
Enabling secure internal and external data sharing through robust governance, agreements and collaboration frameworks
Driving stakeholder engagement, training and cultural change to embed data governance best practice across the Institute
Does this sound like you?
You hold an advanced degree in data science, information management, computer science or a related field
At least five years' experience in data governance, data management or information governance roles
You have successfully led the design and implementation of data governance frameworks in complex organisations
Strong knowledge of data governance best practice and frameworks such as DAMA-DMBOK or DCAM
Solid understanding of Australian and international privacy legislation and research ethics requirements
Experienced in data quality, metadata management and data cataloguing approaches
Confident working with highly diverse stakeholders, from researchers to executive leaders
Able to communicate clearly and effectively, with the ability to influence, educate and build trust

About us

The Victor Chang Cardiac Research Institute (the Institute) is dedicated to finding cures for cardiovascular disease and is one of the most respected heart research facilities in the world. Renowned for the quality of its breakthroughs, the Institute was established in 1994 and has grown to a team of more than 200 researchers from around the globe, working across five divisions.

A global centre-of-excellence, it integrates Cardiac Physiology and Transplantation, Developmental and Stem Cell Biology, Molecular Cardiology and Biophysics, Structural and Computational Biology, as well as Vascular Biology. At the Institute, we translate our discoveries into new diagnostics, prevention, and treatment of cardiovascular disease - reducing the incidence, severity, and impact of heart disease. ​


What you will be proud of

Being part of an Institute that is not only dedicated to finding cures for cardiovascular disease, but one that also strives to ensure our employees and students enjoy a great working environment. We value and are committed to providing a safe working environment that embraces diversity, gender equity and promote flexible working arrangements for all employees.

We encourage Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people to apply. We also welcome applicants with diverse educational backgrounds, professional knowledge, personality types, thinking styles and life experiences. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.

What we offer
Salary packaging (including a $15,900 tax-free component, plus meal and entertainment allowances, holiday accommodation/venue hire plus many more).
Banking & lifestyle benefits with Commonwealth Bank, including lending offers, discounts from participating retailers and financial wellbeing webinars.
Fitness Passport
Excellent environment with new state-of-the-art equipment.
Collaborative team environment
Continuous learning and development opportunities



We are only accepting applications from candidates with full working rights in Australia.

Early applications are encouraged, we are reviewing and conducting interviews on a rolling basis upon receiving applications.



Come and work with us & help make a difference.  ​

Accenture is a global professional services company with leading capabilities in digital, cloud and security. Find out more about us at accenture.com.


WHO WE ARE:​

The CFO&EV team works with clients to tackle issues of importance to the Chief Financial Officer, principally in the development and implementation of new finance operating models, finance organisation strategies, treasury and risk management strategies, and development of new business insight and performance management capabilities. Our analysis, insight and recommendations enable our clients to achieve long-term transformation and growth.

 

THE WORK:

You will be working on significant Transformation in the CFO Agenda across our offerings of Finance Strategy, Value Case, Operating model, Process design, Integrated Planning and Forecasting and Global Business Services

 

Location: Melbourne/ Sydney



HERE’S WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

Proven experience within a top tier consulting firm providing Finance Transformation services for clients

Proven experience in delivering transformation projects to improve Finance functions, including Finance operating models (design to implementation, including Shared Services / Outsourcing), Finance organisation design, Finance process improvement and digitisation (OTC, RTR, PTP, FP&A)

Proven history of working successfully with C-level leadership.

Prior experience in developing and delivering Finance related solutions across industry sectors in Australia

Ability to manage and solve complex business problems

An undergraduate degree is a necessity with qualifications in Finance highly regarded

Knowledge of the Australian Market

Entrepreneurial spirit, excellent communication and stakeholder management skills

Advisory experience into key Finance digital technologies (e.g. SAP, Oracle, Workday, Anaplan, Onestream etc.)

Broad understanding of AI / Generative AI / Agentic and their potential impact on finance processes, with keen interest in emerging trends.

GTDocs Senior Manager + Lawyer


About Gilbert + Tobin

Gilbert + Tobin is built on enduring values of excellence, trust, respect, integrity and good corporate citizenship. We are a leading firm with over 500 lawyers, trusted by our clients to navigate today’s increasingly complex world.

We are purpose-built for our clients’ most complex work - across transactions, disputes and regulation. Our partners are leaders in their fields, delivering the highest quality of legal advice and providing an exceptional edge for our clients. They also provide our lawyers the opportunity to work with and learn from them. We are innovators in the use of technology, redefining value and service in the legal industry.

Our open, merit-based culture attracts and inspires the best legal talent. We remain as determined as ever to be the best it’s possible to be.

The opportunity

This is a senior legal leadership role for a practising or recently practising lawyer who wants to apply legal judgement at scale: leading complex document and evidence workflows, supervising lawyers and managers, and shaping how a top-tier firm uses technology, analytics and market-leading Generative AI tools in live matters.

You will be a senior legal voice in how G+T designs, prices and delivers document- and information-intensive legal work across disputes, investigations, regulatory matters and transactions.

As the Melbourne GTDocs Senior Manager + Lawyer, you will report to the Head of GTDocs, lead and develop the Melbourne GTDocs team, work closely with the Sydney Senior Manager + Lawyer and Legal Informatics.

About GTDocs

GTDocs is an award-winning specialist team within Gilbert + Tobin dedicated to delivering document review, discovery, evidence review and related projects that are agile, cost effective, technology-enabled and consistently high quality.

GTDocs brings together legal judgement, project management, review methodology, eDiscovery, analytics, technology-assisted review, market-leading Generative AI tools and flexible resourcing. We work closely with partners, legal teams, clients, Legal Informatics, Legal Project Management and external provider teams to deliver defensible and commercially sensible outcomes on complex matters.

The team’s work is central to G+T’s strategic response to the changing legal services market. GTDocs helps convert matter delivery experience into better playbooks, quality assurance frameworks, pricing approaches, reporting models and repeatable GenAI-enabled evidence workflows.

About the role

You will be accountable for senior leadership of the Melbourne GTDocs team and for the effective delivery of complex document- and evidence-intensive legal work nationally. The role combines legal supervision, people leadership, client and partner engagement, commercial judgement, review design and continuous improvement.

More broadly, you will:

Lead, coach and develop the Melbourne GTDocs team.
Bring practising legal judgement to the design and supervision of review strategy, relevance, privilege, confidentiality, redaction, production, issue coding, evidence preparation and escalation workflows.
Scope, price, resource and manage complex reviews across disputes, class actions, regulatory investigations, competition and consumer matters, internal investigations, cyber incident responses, due diligence and transaction support.
Work directly with partners, senior lawyers and clients to translate legal issues into practical review protocols, assumptions, dashboards, quality controls, reporting and decision points.
Oversee first-level review, second-level review, quality assurance, privilege review, confidentiality review, redactions, production checks, consistency analysis and risk escalation.
Use review data to give clear, strategic insight on progress, review rates, responsiveness, privilege, quality outcomes, budget, completion risk and options for narrowing or accelerating review.
Partner with Legal Informatics and Legal Project Management to apply the right mix of people, process and technology, including Relativity and other eDiscovery platforms, analytics, technology-assisted review, Generative AI tools like Harvey and approved legal AI workflows.
Use, provide guidance on the use of, and embed market-leading Generative AI tools in review and evidence workflows, including prompt and workflow design, playbooks, guardrails, human-in-the-loop review, evaluation and escalation protocols.
Manage onshore and offshore resourcing, including allocation, supervision and quality control of internal team members, contractors and provider resources.
Create and maintain review playbooks, protocols, templates, training, matter debriefs and reusable process assets so that lessons from live matters improve future delivery.
Act as a senior Melbourne point of contact for GTDocs and help strengthen relationships with practice groups, clients and specialist teams.

About you

This role is designed for a senior lawyer who wants to stay close to substantive legal work while leading a scaled legal delivery capability. You may currently be a Senior Associate, Special Counsel, senior in-house lawyer, senior managed legal services lawyer, or equivalent. You will likely have substantial experience in disputes, investigations, regulatory work, class actions, competition, or another document-heavy practice area.

The successful candidate will be able to demonstrate:

Admission as a lawyer in Australia, or equivalent legal qualification, with a current or recent practising background. A current or eligible Australian practising certificate is strongly preferred.
Around 5+ years’ PQE or equivalent senior experience, with the judgement and credibility to work directly with partners, clients and senior legal teams.
Strong practising experience in disputes, investigations, regulatory matters, class actions, competition, cyber, due diligence, transaction review or other document- and evidence-intensive matters.
Experience leading teams of lawyers, paralegals, analysts or reviewers.
Deep understanding of legal review methodology, including protocols, relevance, issue coding, privilege, confidentiality, redactions, QA, sampling, consistency, production and escalation.
Familiarity with eDiscovery and review platforms such as Relativity, Nuix, Reveal, Canopy or equivalent, and a practical interest in market-leading Generative AI tools for legal work, including Harvey, AI functionality in review platforms or equivalent legal AI tools.
Confidence using, guiding others on and embedding Generative AI tools responsibly in a legal context, including confidentiality, privilege, professional responsibility, verification, auditability and appropriate human supervision.
Commercial acumen in scoping, assumptions, budgets, pricing, resourcing models, delivery economics and client value.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to make complex legal, technical and data-driven issues accessible to partners, clients and review teams.
A practical, calm and accountable leadership style, with the ability to set priorities, keep work moving under pressure and develop high-performing team members.
A continuous improvement mindset: you do not just run the current matter; you build the playbooks, metrics, training and workflows that make the next matter better.

What makes this role different

You will remain connected to complex legal work, but with broader influence than a traditional matter-by-matter fee earner role.
You will lead a Melbourne team and shape a national capability at the intersection of legal judgement, evidence strategy, legal technology, responsible GenAI adoption and AI-enabled service delivery.
You will work with partners and specialist teams on high-value matters where review quality, defensibility, speed, cost certainty and strategic insight matter.
You will help build the next generation of GTDocs delivery: better workflows, better supervision, better data, better pricing, better client experience and better use of market-leading Generative AI tools.

What we offer

Gilbert + Tobin offers a friendly, flexible and professional working environment. In addition to top of market remuneration, we provide benefits such as up to 26 weeks’ paid parental leave, additional annual leave, subsidised café and gym membership, end-of-trip facilities, exclusive retail discounts, social events and more benefits, which you can view here: http://bit.ly/GTFLYER

Our Melbourne office is located in the heart of the CBD at 101 Collins Street, with national collaboration across G+T’s offices and specialist teams.

Please submit your interest via our career’s website with a resume.
  
We are committed to providing and maintaining a diverse and inclusive environment and a culture where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute.
